mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rates signal a disinflationary monetary tightening, which creates uncertainty about corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL falls > 0.5% in 1mo AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of high-GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of high-INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly decline in residential construction (HOUST) signals weak housing demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, which boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 50k in 1mo AND UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of high-RSAFS periods.
